ksa_text: Ability to integrate trustworthy AI principles into technical decision-making, holding validity, reliability, safety, security, resilience, accountability, transparency, explainability, privacy, and fairness as simultaneous design constraints.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Included: ability maps directly to AI Security Specialist scope — RSK-specific signals detected ("ai principles", "explainabil", "transparency"). STRM composite is zero (0.0000 — note: STRM frameworks underweight pure risk vocabulary); note that framework scores routinely underweight risk management and governance vocabulary, so text-based signal carries primary weight here. Seniority is appropriate: the KSA describes practice-level execution, not enterprise strategy.

pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: External validation strong: 3 job-posting-level and 0 certification-level matches (CRISC, CISM, FRM, NIST AI RMF, SR 11-7, IAPP). Competency appears regularly in RSK role descriptions and/or certification curricula.
